Specify Preferences for the Uniface Router Monitor

To control the behavior of the Uniface Router Monitor, you can edit the monitor preferences.

  1. In the Uniface Router Monitor, choose File >Settings.
  2. Set the following preferences, as desired:
    Table 1. Uniface Router Monitor Settings
    Preference Description Initial Default
    Time between updates Frequency at which the Uniface Router is to be polled for dynamic information about the Clients list, the Servers list, and about the current state of the Uniface Router.
    Note: The Uniface Router performance might deteriorate if it is polled too frequently. Updates can overlap if large quantities of data are updated or if calls are slow. You should experiment within your environment to find the best polling rate.
    		 30 seconds
    Connect at start-up Connect the Router Monitor to the Uniface Router at start-up. Supply the default values for Hostname, Network protocol, and Address/port. You can enter some or all values.
    Tip: If you select Store Settings In File URMON.CNF, you can subsequently start up the Router Monitor from the command line, or by using a short-cut, without reentering connection information every time.
    		 On
    Become privileged user Display the Get privileged dialog when the Router Monitor starts. You can enter the default User name to be used. By default, the check box is cleared. Off
    Show dynamic information of URouter Poll the Uniface Router periodically for its dynamic state information for display on the main form. By default, the check box is selected. On
    Show list of servers Display the list of servers according to the specified selection criteria. By default, the check box is selected.
    • All servers—display all servers connected to the current Uniface Router on all host machines.
    • Specific servers—display those servers with the specified Host, User, and/or UST. You can specify some or all values.
    • Servers serving specific clients—display those servers that are serving clients with the specified Host name, User name, and/or UST. You can specify some or all values.
    		 On
    Show list of clients Display the dynamic Clients list according to the specified selection criteria. By default, the check box is selected.
    • All clients—display all clients connected to the current Uniface Router on all host machines.
    • Specific clients—display those clients with the specified Host, User, and/or UST. You can specify some or all values.
    • Clients served by specific servers—display those clients that are being served by servers with the specified Host name, User name, and/or UST. You can specify some or all values.
    		 On
    Store settings in file URMON.CNF Store the settings in the Router Monitor’s configuration file.
    Note: Do not modify urmon.cnf. If you want to monitor several Uniface Routers using different Monitor parameters, create multiple configuration files with different names, each of which you can rename to urmon.cnf just before you run the Router Monitor.

    The default file name is urmon.cnf, but you can change this to a file name and location of your choice in the Router Monitor’s assignment file urmon.asn, for example:

    [FILES] urmon.cnf usysadm:mymon.cnf     		On
  3. Click OK to save the Router Monitor settings. The changes take effect immediately.
Related Links
  • urmon.asn

    Assignment file for the Uniface Router Monitor. You can use the urmon.asn file to specify settings that relate to the Uniface Router Monitor.

  • urouter.asn

    Assignment file for the Uniface Router. You can use the urouter.asn file to specify settings, paths to connectors, and Uniface Server environment definitions, such as USTs.

  • Uniface Server

    A Uniface Server is a server-based process that handles requests for remote data and component execution that it receives from client applications.

  • Uniface Router

    The Uniface Router is a multi-threaded process (urouter) responsible for interprocess communication in Uniface applications. It listens for requests from Uniface client applications and routes them to the appropriate Uniface Server, and it passes messages between various Uniface applications.

  • Uniface Router Monitor

    The Uniface Router Monitor is a Uniface Windows application that you can use to fine-tune the performance of the Uniface Router while it is running.

If you want to monitor several Uniface Routers using different Monitor parameters, create multiple configuration files with different names, each of which you can rename to urmon.cnf just before you run the Router Monitor. The default file name is urmon.cnf , but you can change this to a file name and location of your choice in the Router Monitor’s assignment file urmon.asn , for example: [FILES] urmon.cnf usysadm:mymon.cnf On Click OK to save the Router Monitor settings. The changes take effect immediately. Related Links urmon.asn Assignment file for the Uniface Router Monitor.
